tools.name: find_xkcd
tools.description:
Find xkcd comics semantically relevant to the current conversation.

    Call this whenever an xkcd comic might enrich the conversation — when the
    discussion lands on a topic xkcd is famous for skewering (programming, science,
    statistics, relationships, the absurdity of standards, etc.).

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "find_xkcd" stacks urgency/override words (important, always). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Find xkcd comics semantically relevant to the current conversation. Call this whenever an xkcd comic might enrich the conversation — when the discussion lands o…

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "find_xkcd" appears informational but accepts parameters (context) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: find_xkcd · Public sink: find_xkcd.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: find_xkcd · Public sink: find_xkcd

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
